methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

private.name: Matic Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Shinkei is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Saif Khawaja and Reed Ginsberg that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of three late-stage private peers in the consumer tech space (Matic Robotics, Daylight Computer, and Whisper Aero); no public comparables exist in the immediate sector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach given data availability. Using the $30M total raised as a valuation proxy, a typical Series A to subsequent-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $60M. This method anchors the analysis by capturing the capital intensity and milestone-based value creation typical of the stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. Given the limited methodological basis—only one triangulation method is applicable with current data—this range reflects the inherent uncertainty in early-stage consumer tech valuations where revenue and unit economics may still be evolving. Key uncertainties include customer acquisition costs, retention dynamics, and path to profitability relative to the private peer set. Validation against the three comparable companies' recent fundraise terms and growth metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
